evidenceSummary.excerpt: Pin-to-pin P2P drop-in for the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. Nexperia BSN20 (Diodes Incorporated 7-inch reel variant) is a 50 V N-channel enhancement mode MOSFET in SOT-23-3 with identical pinout to BSS138L. Logic-level gate drive (RDS(on) ~1.8 Ω at Vgs = 10 V), 0.18 A ID, 360 mW Pd.

fitNotes: Functional alternative to the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. 2N7002K-7 (Nexperia / Diodes Incorporated) is a 60 V N-channel logic-level enhancement mode MOSFET in SOT-23-3 with the same pinout (1=Gate, 2=Drain, 3=Source). 0.115 A ID and ~3 Ω RDS(on) at Vgs = 10 V. Same logic-level gate drive and small-signal switching application class as BSS138L, but ~2x lower current rating. Use as drop-in when the design's continuous drain current is below 100 mA (level-shifting, load switch, simple analog switches).
replacementCandidates.productOverview: The 2N7002K-7 is a N-channel MOSFET (Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor) manufactured by Diodes Incorporated. It is designed for low-voltage applications and is widely used in various electronic circuits due to its efficiency and reliability. Key Features: Type : N-channel MOSFET Package : The 2N7002K-7 is typically available in a SOT-23 package, which is a small, surface-mount package that allows for efficient use of board space. Voltage Rating : The device has a maximum drain-source voltage (V_DS) of 60V, making it suitable for a variety of applications that require moderate voltage handling. Current Rating : It can handle a continuous drain current (I_D) of up to 200mA, which is adequate for many low-power switching applications. Gate Threshold Voltage (V_GS(th)) : The gate threshold voltage is typically in the range of 0.8V to 3V, allowing it to be driven by low-voltage logic levels. R_DS(on) : The on-resistance (R_DS(on)) is low, typically around 0.5 ohms at a gate-source voltage (V_GS) of 10V, which contributes to lower power losses during operation. Switching Speed : The 2N7002K-7 features fast switching capabilities, making it suitable for high-speed applications such as switching power supplies and signal switching. Thermal Characteristics : The device has a thermal resistance (RθJA) that allows it to operate efficiently without overheating, provided it is used within its specified limits. Applications: The 2N7002K-7 is commonly used in various applications, including: Switching Regulators : It can be used in DC-DC converters and other power management circuits. Signal Switching : Ideal for switching signals in digital circuits, such as microcontroller outputs. Load Switching : Suitable for controlling loads in automotive and industrial applications. Relay Replacement : Can be used to replace mechanical relays in low-power applications for faster switching and increased reliability. Electrical Characteristics: Maximum Gate-Source Voltage (V_GS) : ±20V Maximum Power Dissipation (P_D) : Typically around 300mW, depending on the thermal management of the PCB. Operating Temperature Range : The device can operate in a temperature range from -55°C to +150°C, making it suitable for a variety of environmental conditions. Conclusion: The 2N7002K-7 from Diodes Incorporated is a versatile and efficient N-channel MOSFET that is well-suited for low-voltage applications. Its compact size, low on-resistance, and fast switching capabilities make it a popular choice among engineers and designers for a wide range of electronic circuits.

Same logic-level gate drive and small-signal switching application class as BSS138L, but ~2x lower current rating. Use as drop-in when the design's continuous drain current is below 100 mA (level-shifting, load switch, simple analog switches).

fitNotes: Pin-to-pin P2P drop-in for the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. Nexperia BSN20 (Diodes Incorporated 7-inch reel variant) is a 50 V N-channel enhancement mode MOSFET in SOT-23-3 with identical pinout to BSS138L. Logic-level gate drive (RDS(on) ~1.8 Ω at Vgs = 10 V), 0.18 A ID, 360 mW Pd. Same 50 V VDS class and SOT-23-3 footprint. Recommended when the design needs lower RDS(on) for reduced conduction loss vs BSS138L.

replacementCandidates.description: N-channel logic-level enhancement mode MOSFET, 60 V, 0.23 A, SOT-23-3. Pin-to-pin drop-in replacement for onsemi BSS138L.
fitNotes: Pin-to-pin P2P drop-in for the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. Infineon BSS138N is the SIPMOS small-signal N-channel MOSFET in PG-SOT-23 (3-pin) with identical SOT-23-3 footprint and pinout (1=Gate, 2=Drain, 3=Source). Same 3.5 Ω RDS(on) at the same gate drive — exact parametric replacement per Ovaga and DigiKey 'Similar' listings. 60 V VDS (vs BSS138L's 50 V) provides 20% extra voltage headroom. 0.23 A ID vs BSS138L's 0.20 A. Recommended primary drop-in for new designs after BSS138L is obsolete.
replacementCandidates.productOverview: BSS138N is Infineon Technologies' SIPMOS small-signal N-channel enhancement mode MOSFET in PG-SOT-23 (3-pin). Rated 60 V VDS, 0.23 A ID, 3.5 Ω Rds(on) at Vgs = 10 V, 360 mW Pd. Pin-to-pin drop-in for onsemi BSS138L (SOT-23-3 footprint, identical pinout 1=Gate, 2=Drain, 3=Source). Identical RDS(on) (3.5 Ω) at the same gate drive as onsemi BSS138L, making it an exact parametric replacement per Ovaga cross-reference and DigiKey 'Similar' listing. Marketed as the Infineon successor to the BSS138 footprint.
replacementCandidates.reason: Pin-to-pin P2P drop-in for the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. Infineon BSS138N is the SIPMOS small-signal N-channel MOSFET in PG-SOT-23 (3-pin) with identical SOT-23-3 footprint and pinout (1=Gate, 2=Drain, 3=Source). Same 3.5 Ω RDS(on) at the same gate drive — exact parametric replacement per Ovaga and DigiKey 'Similar' listings. 60 V VDS (vs BSS138L's 50 V) provides 20% extra voltage headroom. 0.23 A ID vs BSS138L's 0.20 A. Recommended primary drop-in for new designs after BSS138L is obsolete.

fitNotes: Pin-to-pin P2P drop-in for the obsolete onsemi BSS138L. Identical SOT-23-3 footprint and pinout (1=Gate, 2=Drain, 3=Source). Same 50 V VDS, 200 mA ID, 3.5 ohm Rds(on) ratings. onsemi product page (2025) shows BSS138L lifecycle as 'Last Shipments' (the onsemi glossary defines this as the final 6-month shipment window before Obsolete), and DigiKey lists the part as 'Obsolete'. Diodes BSS138-7-F is the universal cross-vendor BSS138 drop-in listed as the primary parametric equivalent on Z2Data and Octopart.
replacementCandidates.productOverview: The BSS138-7-F is a N-channel MOSFET (Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor) manufactured by Diodes Incorporated. It is designed for low-voltage applications and is commonly used in switching and amplification circuits. Here’s a detailed description of its key features and specifications: General Characteristics: Type : N-channel MOSFET Package : Typically available in a small SOT-23 package, which is a surface-mount device (SMD) that allows for efficient use of PCB space. Pin Configuration : The SOT-23 package usually has three pins, which are designated as Gate (G), Drain (D), and Source (S). Electrical Specifications: Maximum Drain-Source Voltage (V_DS) : The BSS138 can handle a maximum V_DS of 50V, making it suitable for a variety of applications where moderate voltage levels are present. Continuous Drain Current (I_D) : It can support a continuous drain current of up to 220 mA at a temperature of 25°C, which is adequate for many low-power applications. Gate Threshold Voltage (V_GS(th)) : The gate threshold voltage typically ranges from 0.8V to 1.5V, indicating the minimum gate-to-source voltage required to turn the MOSFET on. R_DS(on) : The on-resistance (R_DS(on)) is typically around 3.5 ohms at a V_GS of 10V, which contributes to lower power losses during operation. Performance Characteristics: Switching Speed : The BSS138 is known for its fast switching capabilities, making it suitable for high-speed applications. Thermal Resistance : The thermal resistance from junction to ambient (RθJA) is relatively low, allowing for efficient heat dissipation during operation. Safe Operating Area (SOA) : The device is designed to operate within a safe area, ensuring reliability and longevity in various applications. Applications: The BSS138-7-F is widely used in: Switching Regulators : For efficient power management in DC-DC converters. Level Shifting : In interfacing between different voltage levels in digital circuits. Signal Switching : For controlling signals in various electronic devices. Load Switching : In applications where it is necessary to control larger loads with a smaller control signal. Additional Features: RoHS Compliant : The BSS138-7-F is compliant with the Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) directive, making it environmentally friendly. Low Gate Drive Power : The device requires minimal gate drive power, which is advantageous in battery-operated devices. Conclusion: The BSS138-7-F from Diodes Incorporated is a versatile and efficient N-channel MOSFET suitable for a wide range of applications, particularly in low-voltage and low-power environments. Its compact size, combined with its robust electrical characteristics, makes it a popular choice among engineers and designers in the electronics industry.

Product Attribute.Lifecycle Status: Last Shipments (entering Obsolete, per onsemi 2025 product page)

target.description: Single N-channel logic-level enhancement mode MOSFET, 50 V, 200 mA, SOT-23-3. onsemi lifecycle status: 'Last Shipments' (entering Obsolete).
target.productOverview: BSS138L is onsemi's SOT-23-3 N-channel logic-level enhancement mode MOSFET rated 50 V VDS, 200 mA ID, 3.5 Ω Rds(on) at Vgs = 4.5 V, 350 mW Pd. onsemi product page (2025) shows lifecycle status 'Last Shipments' — the onsemi lifecycle glossary defines 'Last Shipments' as a 6-month period of final shipments, after which the part becomes Obsolete. DigiKey lists Part Status as 'Obsolete' for BSS138L. Direct P2P drop-in: Diodes Incorporated BSS138-7-F (SOT-23-3, 50 V, 200 mA, 3.5 Ω Rds(on)) — identical pinout and pin-for-pin spec match.
